operator: Orphan well (1948 Gulf Oil legacy); injection pressure from Goodnight Midstream Permian + Blackbeard Operating
source: Karanam et al. 2024 Geophys. Res. Lett.; Texas Monthly; Tubbs Corner gazetteer 31.3826°N 102.6171°W
description: Meister West Ranch. Abandoned 1948 Gulf Oil dry hole (1,390 ft), brine erupted 30m for ~2 weeks. ~357,000 BBL cumulative (15M gallons). Chlorides 174,000 ppm. Injection-induced: pressure migrated 12 mi from 9 SWD wells. SkyGeo InSAR detected uplift 1.5 yr prior. Second surface event (fissures) began within 1 year nearby.
spill_footprint_note: Surface expression documented; 500m bbox captures salinized zone. Use 2022-01 to 2022-06 for peak signal.

source: First Alert 7 Dec 2023; Marfa Public Radio Jan/Feb 2024; Big Bend Sentinel Jan 2024
description: FM 329, Bill Wright Ranch. ~0.5 mi from Tubbs Corner 2022 event. 100-ft brine geyser, ~13,000 gal/hr for 45 days. Chlorides 154,000 ppm. ~30 acres vegetation kill. RRC plugging completed Jan 29 2024 ($2.5M). 'This well is on no map' — RRC Director Danny Sorrells.
spill_footprint_note: 30-acre kill zone confirmed. Check Dec 7 2023 – Feb 2024 imagery window.

source: Inside Climate News Oct 2023; Texas Tribune Oct 2023; TRRC inspection records
description: ~10 miles north of Balmorhea. 77,500 BBL tank battery spill. Affected area ~0.25 × 0.5 mi. Heavy salt crystals and distressed vegetation on 3 RRC inspections (Jul, Sep, Nov 2020). Spill pre-dates 2021 cutoff but salt/vegetation damage persists in 2021+ imagery.
spill_footprint_note: Persistent salt deposition and veg kill. Use 2021-01 onwards for residual signature detection.

source: NMOCD deferral request APP_82711_513008; Section 36 T22S R28E Eddy County — exact GPS in filing
description: Pinnacle State #035H. Valve left open (human error). 7 BBL produced water + 7 BBL crude; ~5 BBL each recovered. ~300 cu yd contaminated soil removed Nov 1 2021. Deferral denied Jan 2022.
spill_footprint_note: Very small volume — useful as LOW-SIGNAL calibration point. Should NOT trigger strong APEX/HPWI response.

operator: Orphan well — Sloan Blair No. 1 (1951, originally Gulf Oil dry hole)
source: Wikipedia Lake Boehmer: 31°13'25.3"N 102°43'43.7"W; Texas Standard Apr 2024; Marfa Public Radio Apr 2024
description: ~25 mi north of Fort Stockton, off FM 1053 near Imperial TX. Ongoing brine discharge for 20+ years; 60-acre artificial saltwater lake. TCEQ/RRC jurisdiction dispute. Continuous leak — use any 2021+ imagery for reliable detection.
spill_footprint_note: HIGHEST CONFIDENCE DETECTION SITE. 60-acre persistent saltwater lake. Any date 2021+ should show strong SWIR brine signal.

operator: Kinder Morgan (well drilled 1961 by Texas El Paso Gas, 11,331 ft)
source: DeSmog Oct 2024; Texas Tribune Oct 10 & 22 2024; Big Bend Sentinel Oct 2024
description: 5 mi west of Toyah (pop. 61). 100-ft brine geyser with H2S (rotten-egg detected 2 mi downwind). Continuous blowout Oct 2–21 (19 days). Sealed Oct 21 2024. RRC initially would not confirm well owner.
spill_footprint_note: Active Oct 2–21 2024. Check Oct 5–25 S2 acquisitions for peak signal.

source: Inside Climate News May 2023; Pipeline Safety Trust; EPA OSC response.epa.gov/EnlinkMidstream
description: El Dorado Crude Station, 11 mi south of Midland. 9,583 BBL crude oil (402,486 gallons), only 720 BBL recovered. Operator overpressurized 12-inch pipeline; leak detection alarm ignored ~3 hrs. Largest crude spill in Permian since 2010. PHMSA investigation. NOTE: crude oil, not produced water — useful for hydrocarbon index (HCAI) calibration.
spill_footprint_note: Large crude spill — HCAI/FBC signal expected, NOT brine indices. Include as crude-vs-brine discrimination test.

source: NM Political Report Mar 2024; EMNRD NOV doc; $2,007,900 civil penalty
description: ~16 mi south of Carlsbad, two well sites. Violations from under-reported spills; $2M+ EMNRD penalty. Low volume but chronic and deliberate non-reporting.
spill_footprint_note: Low coordinate confidence (~2 km). Small volume. Best used as likely-negative calibration point.
